Option.Description: The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.
The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.

Command.Name: dailyCosts
Command.Description: Show the daily cost by a given dimension.

Option.Description: The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The grouping to use. E.g. ResourceGroupName, Meter, ResourceLocation, etc. Defaults to ResourceGroupName.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.

Command.Name: costByResource
Command.Description: Show the cost details by resource.

Option.Description: The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Exclude meter details from the output.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.

Command.Name: costByTag
Command.Description: Show the cost details by the provided tag key(s).

Option.Description: The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The tags to return, for example: Cost Center or Owner. You can specify multiple tags by using the --tag option multiple times.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.
The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The grouping to use. E.g. ResourceGroupName, Meter, ResourceLocation, etc. Defaults to ResourceGroupName.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The number of days to use for recent activity. Defaults to 7.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
The significant change in cost to use. Defaults to 0.75 (75%).
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The number of days to use for steady growth. Defaults to 7.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The thresshold cost to use. Values lower than this are excluded. Defaults to 2.00.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.
The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.
The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.

Command.Name: region
Command.Description: Run what-if scenarios to check price differences if the resources would have run in a different region. Only applies to VMs.

Option.Description: The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.
The billing account id to use.
The base address for the Cost API. Defaults to https://management.azure.com/
Increase logging verbosity to show all debug logs.
The enrollment account id to use.
Filter the output by the specified properties. Defaults to no filtering and can be multiple values.
The start date to use for the costs. Defaults to the first day of the previous month.
Allows overriding the default HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 100 seconds.

Include Tags from the selected dimension. The option is used for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Valid only for DailyCost report and output to Json, JsonC or Csv. Ignored in other reports and output formats.
The metric to use for the costs. Defaults to ActualCost. (ActualCost, AmortizedCost)
The number of items to show before collapsing the rest into an 'Others' item.
The output format to use. Defaults to Console (Console, Json, JsonC, Text, Markdown, Csv)
The base address for the Price API. Defaults to https://prices.azure.com/
JMESPath query string, applicable for the Json output only. See http://jmespath.org/ for more information and examples.
The resource group to scope the request to. Need to be used in combination with the subscription id.
Skip header creation for specific output formats. Useful when appending the output from multiple runs into one file. Defaults to false.
The subscription id to use. Will try to fetch the active id if not specified.
The timeframe to use for the costs. Defaults to BillingMonthToDate. When set to Custom, specify the from and to dates using the --from and --to options
The end date to use for the costs. Defaults to the current date.
Force the use of USD for the currency. Defaults to false to use the currency returned by the API.
